rows: ・ Damage equal to 1/8 of its max HP is taken at the end of every turn

・ Increasing poison damage is taken at the end of every turn, starting at 1/16 of its max HP , then 2/16, then 3/16, and so on ・Damage restarts at 1/16 of its max HP if the Pokemon switches out, then returns onto the field
・ Damage equal to 1/16 of its max HP is taken at the end of every turn ・Damage output of its physical moves is also halved
・ 12.5% chance of being unable to use moves ・ Its Speed stat is also halved .

・ The Pokemon cannot move ・ 25% chance of being cured each time it tries to execute a move ・ The Pokemon is cured on its third turn after becoming frozen ・ The Pokemon can thaw out if it uses or takes damage from a Fire-type move or certain other moves.

・ The Pokemon can't use moves other than Snore or Sleep Talk ・ After a Pokemon is put to sleep, it will remain asleep on its first turn ・ It will have a 1-in-3 chance of being cured on its second turn ・ The Pokemon will be cured on its third turn ・ Sleep from the move Rest has no chance of being cured until its third turn

content.text: There are five major status conditions in the game: poison, burn, paralysis, freeze, and sleep. However, a poisoned Pokemon can be either poisoned or badly poisoned, which affects the amount of damage it will take each turn.
A Pokemon can only have one major status condition active at a time. If you try to inflict a status condition on a Pokemon that already has a status effect active, it will not work.

rows: ・ The Pokemon may deal damage to itself during battle instead of using its chosen move ・ Can wear off naturally after a few turns ・ Own Tempo and Tangled Feet Pokemon are immune to confusion

・ The Pokemon takes damage equal to 1/4 of its max HP every turn ・ Only inflicted when a Ghost-type Pokemon uses the move Curse . No status condition is inflicted if a non-Ghost Type uses the move Curse.

・ The Pokemon may refuse to attack the opposing Pokemon due to being immobilized by love ・ Infatuation ends when either the infatuated Pokemon or the Pokemon it's infatuated with is switched out of battle ・ Caused by using Attract or the Cute Charm Ability on a Pokemon of the opposite gender ・ Oblivious Pokemon are immune to infatuation
・ The Pokemon cannot move for that turn ・ Caused by a Pokemon that moved before it during the same turn ・ Inner Focus and Steadfast Pokemon cannot be flinched
・ The Pokemon cannot use status moves

・ The Pokemon cannot be switched out for up to 5 turns ・ The Pokemon takes damage equal to 1/8 of its max HP at the end of every turn ・ Caused by moves such as Bind , Fire Spin , Infestation , Sand Tomb , Snap Trap , Whirlpool , and Wrap ・ Rapid Spin and Mortal Spin removes the bound status
・ The Pokemon takes damage equal to 1/16 of its max HP at the end of every turn, and this damage is absorbed by the opposing Pokemon
・ The Pokemon takes damage equal to 1/16 of its max HP at the end of every turn ・ For Water- and Steel-type Pokemon , they take damage equal to 1/8 of their max HP at the end of every turn

content.text: Hidden status conditions are not listed on the Pokemon's HP bar when they're in effect. However, they do appear in the Battle Info section when you view it during the battle.

These hidden status conditions tend to be easier to remove than major status conditions, requiring to you just switch out in battle or wait a few turns. Hidden status conditions can occur simultaneously with major status conditions or with other hidden status conditions.

rows: Pokemon with lower Speed stats move first.
Halves the damage taken from physical moves . In Double Battles, this damage is reduced by 1/3 instead.
Halves the damage taken from special moves . In Double Battles, this damage is reduced by 1/3 instead.
Halves the damage taken from physical and special moves. In Double Battles, this damage is reduced by 1/3 instead.
Pokemon that switch into battle will take damage equal to 1/8 of their max HP . This damage differs depending on the Pokemon's type matchup with the Rock type .

Pokemon that switch into battle will take damage equal to 1/8 of their max HP . This damage will increase if 2 layers of Spikes are set, and again if 3 layers are set. Pokemon such as Flying types or ones with the Levitate Ability are unaffected.
Pokemon that switch into battle will be poisoned or, if 2 layers of Toxic Spikes are set, badly poisoned . Pokemon such as Flying types or ones with the Levitate Ability are unaffected.

content.text: These are the common battle statuses that you can encounter in Ranked Battles . Entry hazards such as Spikes and Stealth Rocks are more common in Single Battles, while defensive and positional battle effects such as Trick Room, Tailwind, and screens are more common in Double Battles.

Unlike status conditions that affect individual Pokemon, battle statuses affect an entire side of the battlefield . All Pokemon that appear on the side where the battle status is in effect will be covered by it, even if the Pokemon get switched out . The active status effects can be viewed when checking the Battle Info during a match.

rows: Equip a Lum Berry Use Status-Curing Moves Certain Abilities Can Cure Status Switch Out Your Pokemon
Cures any status condition or confusion. Disappears for the duration of the battle after a single use.

content.text: Using a Lum Berry is the most accessible way for any Pokemon to cure their status condition. There are other Berries heal specific status conditions—such as Pecha Berry for poison—but if you're looking to avoid any possible status on your Pokemon, it's best to equip a Lum Berry.

Take note that this means that the Lum Berry will occupy your Pokemon's Held Item slot. This means that you cannot equip it with a more offense-oriented item. You also cannot equip the same item on multiple Pokemon if you're participating in Ranked Battles, so be mindful when assigning this item to a Pokemon.

Move Type Acc PP Details Heal Bell Normal - 8 The user cures the status conditions of all the Pokémon in its party, including itself, as well as its allies. Healing Wish Psychic - 12 The user faints. The Pokémon taking its place will have its HP fully restored and its status conditions cured. Rest Psychic - 8 Fully restores the user's HP and cures any status conditions. The user falls asleep for 2 turns. This move fails if the user's HP is full.
There are moves that you can give your Pokemon that they can use to heal themselves or their allies from status conditions.

Rest is the most accessible move for most Pokemon, though it also inflicts your Pokemon with the sleep status condition as well. The sleep status overwrites any existing status condition and heals your Pokemon back up to full HP.

rows: Effect : The Pokémon has a 50% chance of curing the status conditions of its allies at the end of every turn. Learned by :
Effect : Cures the Pokémon's status conditions at the end of every turn in rain. Learned by :
Effect : The Pokémon's status conditions are cured when it switches out of battle. Learned by :
Effect : The Pokémon has a 30% chance of curing its own status conditions at the end of every turn. Learned by :

content.text: There are Abilities in the game that can heal status conditions, some of which require specific conditions. There are also Abilities that are immune to status conditions.
List of Abilities
Switching out your Pokemon can allow hidden status conditions such as confusion or infatuation to be removed from your Pokemon.
However, switching does nothing to Pokemon with a major status condition unless they have an ability that allows them to heal when switched such as Natural Cure .
When to Switch in Battle

rows: Set Up Misty Terrain Use Safeguard Abilities Immune to Status Conditions Types Immune to Status Conditions

content.text: Healing status conditions tend to be more difficult or inconvenient than simply preventing them from happening. The best way to prevent status conditions from disrupting your team is to set up the Misty Terrain .

Misty Terrain prevents all Pokemon within it from being affected by status conditions. However, Pokemon that aren't touching the ground such as Flying types or Pokemon with Levitate are unaffected. Misty Terrain also does not affect field-wide battle status effects such as Trick Room or Spikes.
Misty Terrain Effect and Pokemon That Learn It
Move Type Acc PP Details Safeguard Normal - 20 Gives the user's side the Safeguard status for 5 turns, where the Pokémon is immune to status conditions and cannot become confused.
Safeguard works similarly to Misty Terrain, but it prevent status conditions from afflicting only your team for 5 turns.

rows: Effect : The Pokémon cannot be poisoned or badly poisoned. Learned by :
Effect : The Pokémon cannot become drowsy or be put to sleep. Learned by :
Effect : The Pokémon cannot be paralyzed. Learned by :
Effect : The Pokémon cannot be frozen. Learned by :
Effect : If poisoned or badly poisoned, the Pokémon has 1/8 of its max HP restored at the end of every turn instead of losing HP. Learned by :
Effect : The Pokémon cannot become drowsy or be put to sleep. Learned by :
Effect : Halves the damage the Pokémon takes from Fire-type moves and doubles the power of its Water-type moves. The Pokémon cannot be burned. Learned by :

content.text: There are Pokemon Abilities that are immune to the effects of one or two specific status conditions. If you have these Pokemon on your team, they're great to switch into if you predict that your opponent will use a status-inflicting move on your Pokemon.
Certain Pokemon types have a natural immunity to certain status conditions—usually the ones closely related to their type.
Although Poison and Steel-type Pokemon are generally immune to being poisoned or badly poisoned, they can still be poisoned by Pokemon with the Corrosion ability.

The most common way to inflict status conditions, either on an ally or an opponent, is to use status-inflicting moves. You can use status moves if you only want the status condition without dealing additional damage to your opponent, or you can use an offensive move that has a secondary effect of inflicting a status condtition.
List of Status Inflicting Moves

rows: Effect : When the Pokémon is hit by a contact move, the attacker has a 30% chance of being burned. Learned by :
Effect : When the Pokémon is hit by a contact move, the attacker has a 30% chance of being poisoned. Learned by :
Effect : When the Pokémon hits a target with a contact move, the target has a 30% chance of being poisoned. Learned by :
Effect : When the Pokémon is hit by a contact move, the attacker has a 30% chance of being paralyzed. Learned by :

content.text: Some Abilities can inflict status conditions when making contact with another Pokemon. Contact moves are usually required in order to have a chance of triggering these Abilities.

As of the first regulation of Pokemon Champions, there are currently no items available that can inflict status conditions. However, it is expected that these items will make a return in future regulations.
Items such as Flame Orb or Toxic Orb can inflict status conditions on the holder, which is ideal for activating certain Abilities such as Guts , Marvel Scal e, or Poison Heal .

content.text: Listed above are all the Terrains and their effects. They only affect Pokemon on the ground . This means Flying-type Pokemon and Pokemon with Levitate cannot be affected by them unless they become grounded.
When a Terrain is activated, it will last for 5 turns .

terrain.title: How to Set and Remove Terrain

content.text: Listed above are all the Moves that can set up Terrain. Since the order that moves are used are affected by a Pokemon's Speed stat , it is best to carefully think about what Pokemon you can use to set the Terrain.

If you wish to activate a Terrain faster to assist your team in the same turn, you may use a faster Pokemon. However, if you are battling against another team that utilizes a Terrain, you may want to use a slower Pokemon to override their Terrain.
Listed above are all the currently available Moves that can remove Terrain. You may use this to counter opponents or to shift your strategy.

terrain.title: Moves and Abilities Affected By Terrain

rows: Affected Moves Terrain-Immune Moves Affected Abilities
· Bulldoze * · Earthquake * · Grassy Glide · Terrain Pulse

content.text: *These moves are negatively affected by the Terrain.
Listed above are all the moves affected by Terrain. They may have changes in damage and priority if a certain Terrain is active.

terrain.title: Moves That Temporarily Grant Terrain Immunity

content.text: Listed above are two-turn Moves that turn the user mostly invincible on its first turn of usage . When the Pokemon is in this invincible state, it cannot be affected by Terrain effects .

rows: The Pokémon's type changes depending on the terrain.
Doubles the Pokémon's Speed stat on Electric Terrain.

content.text: Listed above are all the available Abilities that are affected by Terrain. Depending on the Pokemon added to Champions in future updates, similar Abilities may eventually be added.

content.text: These are the Pokemon that have had valuable moves removed from their move lists or have had significant nerfs to their signature moves or abilities, such as Metagross's Heavy Slam.
Here are some of the newly buffed Pokémon. In particular, Swampert's acquisition of Wave Crash is likely to have a significant impact on the competitive gameplay.
15 new items have been added and all of them can be purchased with at the in-game shop.
List of All Newly-Added Items in Regulation M-B

changes.title: List of Changes from Previous Games

rows: Previous Effect : Pokemon have a 25% chance to be unable to move for a turn. Halves speed. New Effect : Pokemon have a 12.5% chance to be unable to move for a turn. Halves speed.

Previous Effect : Pokemon have a 25% chance to thaw when they move each turn. Can be thawed immediately by certain Fire-type moves . New Effect : Pokemon have a 25% chance to thaw when they move each turn but will be guaranteed to thaw by the 3rd turn. Can be thawed immediately by certain Fire-type moves.
Previous Effect : Fully restores HP and puts the Pokemon to sleep for 2 turns. New Effect : Fully restores HP and puts the Pokemon to sleep for 3 turns.

content.text: Some status conditions have undergone changes in Pokemon Champions—the most significant of which is the Freeze status condition. In previous games, a Pokemon could remain frozen for the entirety of the battle, but now it is guaranteed to thaw out by the third turn, similar to the Sleep status effect.
All Status Conditions and Effects Explained

rows: Previous Effect : If both players have the same number of Pokemon once the timer ends, the player with the highest percentage of total remaining HP wins. New Effect : The battle ends in a draw if the battle timer ends.

content.text: If a match goes to timer, the game now automatically ends in a draw . The Pokemon count nor the remaining HP for each Pokemon are no longer considered.

rows: Power has been increased, from 80 to 90.
Power has been increased, from 100 to 120.
Power has been increased, from 25 to 30.

content.text: Not including changes to PP values, there are a lot of moves that have been updated in Pokemon Champions. Many of them increased in base power, though some moves have had their additional effects nerfed.
List of All Moves

rows: Instead of dealing full damage when using contact moves on protecting Pokemon, Unseen Fist now only allows 1/4 of the original damage.

content.text: The Unseen Fist ability has been nerfed. It now only deals 1/4 damage through protecting moves (e.g. Protect , Detect , King's Shield ).
List of Abilities
These are the Pokemon that have gained new moves in Pokemon Champions or relearned past moves that were removed in the previous generation of games.

rows: New Moves : Close Combat Mach Punch U-turn Triple Axel Removed Move :
